_readme: Task scheduler file for Instagram Recipes app. See SCHEDULER_PROMPT.md for agent instructions.

tasks.title: Initialize Expo project with TypeScript
tasks.description: Run `npx create-expo-app@latest InstagramRecipes --template blank-typescript` inside the repo root. Move generated files into the repo root (not a subdirectory). Verify `app.json`, `tsconfig.json`, and `package.json` exist. Commit the result.
tasks.success_criteria: package.json exists with expo as a dependency, tsconfig.json exists extending expo/tsconfig.base, app.json exists with the app name set to InstagramRecipes.

tasks.title: Install and configure all dependencies
tasks.description: Install: `react-native-mmkv`, `zustand`, `nativewind`, `tailwindcss`, `expo-image`, `expo-clipboard`, `expo-video-thumbnails`, `expo-file-system`, `@anthropic-ai/sdk`. Also install dev deps: `@types/react`, `prettier`. Run `npx expo install` for expo-managed packages. Commit package.json and package-lock/yarn.lock.
tasks.success_criteria: All packages listed above appear in package.json dependencies. No install errors.

attempts.agent_note: Completed successfully. Used npm install directly due to expo install network issues.

tasks.title: Configure NativeWind (Tailwind CSS for React Native)
tasks.description: Create `tailwind.config.js` with content paths covering `./app/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}` and `./src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}`. Update `babel.config.js` to include `nativewind/babel` preset. Update `metro.config.js` (create if missing) to use withNativeWind. Add `/// <reference types="nativewind/types" />` to a global type declaration file. Commit all config files.
tasks.success_criteria: tailwind.config.js exists, babel.config.js includes nativewind/babel, metro.config.js exists with withNativeWind wrapper.

tasks.title: Set up Expo Router navigation skeleton
tasks.description: Create the full app/ directory structure per IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md: `app/_layout.tsx` (root Stack layout), `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` (bottom tab navigator with Home, Boards, Settings tabs), `app/(tabs)/index.tsx` (Home placeholder), `app/(tabs)/boards.tsx` (Boards placeholder), `app/(tabs)/settings.tsx` (Settings placeholder), `app/add-recipe.tsx` (Add Recipe placeholder), `app/recipe/[id].tsx` (Recipe Detail placeholder), `app/board/[id].tsx` (Board Detail placeholder). Each screen should render a View with a Text label so navigation can be verified. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: All 8 files exist under app/. Each exports a default React component. _layout files configure the correct navigators.

tasks.title: Define TypeScript data models
tasks.description: Create `src/types/recipe.ts` with interfaces: Recipe, Ingredient, Instruction, Board â€” exactly as specified in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md data models section. Include the `extractionSource` field on Recipe. Export all interfaces. Also create `src/utils/constants.ts` with placeholder constants (APP_NAME, CLAUDE_MODEL = 'claude-opus-4-6'). Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: src/types/recipe.ts exports Recipe, Ingredient, Instruction, Board interfaces. All fields from the plan are present. No TypeScript errors.

tasks.title: Set up MMKV storage and Zustand stores
tasks.description: Create `src/utils/storage.ts` that initialises and exports a single MMKV instance. Create `src/stores/recipe-store.ts` â€” a Zustand store (with persist middleware backed by MMKV) that holds `recipes: Recipe[]` and exposes actions: `addRecipe`, `updateRecipe`, `deleteRecipe`, `getRecipeById`. Create `src/stores/board-store.ts` â€” a Zustand store (with persist) that holds `boards: Board[]` and exposes: `addBoard`, `updateBoard`, `deleteBoard`, `addRecipeToBoard`, `removeRecipeFromBoard`. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Both store files exist, compile without TypeScript errors, use MMKV-backed persist middleware, and export typed hooks.

tasks.title: Build URLInput component with clipboard detection
tasks.description: Create `src/components/URLInput.tsx`. This component renders a text input styled with NativeWind. On focus/mount it reads the clipboard via `expo-clipboard` â€” if the clipboard contains a string matching an Instagram URL pattern (`instagram.com/p/` or `instagram.com/reel/`) it auto-fills the input and shows a 'Paste from clipboard' suggestion. Exposes `onSubmit(url: string)` prop. Includes a 'Fetch Recipe' button. Create `src/hooks/useClipboard.ts` hook that encapsulates clipboard read logic. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: URLInput.tsx and useClipboard.ts exist. Component compiles. Clipboard hook correctly identifies Instagram URL patterns.

tasks.title: Set up Facebook App for Instagram oEmbed API
tasks.description: HUMAN ACTION REQUIRED: Register a Facebook App at developers.facebook.com. Enable the oEmbed Read product. Obtain an App ID and App Secret. Generate an app access token (App ID|App Secret format or via token endpoint). Store these as environment variables in a `.env` file: EXPO_PUBLIC_FACEBOOK_APP_ID and EXPO_PUBLIC_FACEBOOK_APP_SECRET. Add .env to .gitignore.
tasks.success_criteria: Human has a valid Facebook App ID and app access token that can successfully call the Instagram oEmbed endpoint. .env file exists locally (not committed).

tasks.title: Implement Instagram oEmbed fetcher
tasks.description: Create `src/services/instagram-oembed.ts`. Implement `fetchViaOEmbed(url: string): Promise<RawInstagramPost>` where RawInstagramPost = { caption?: string; imageUrl?: string; authorName?: string; thumbnailUrl?: string; html?: string }. Call `https://graph.facebook.com/v22.0/instagram_oembed?url={url}&access_token={token}&fields=thumbnail_url,author_name`. Parse the HTML field to extract caption text if not returned directly (use a simple regex to find text between <blockquote> tags). Return null on failure. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Service file exists and compiles. Function signature matches expected interface. Error cases return null rather than throwing.

tasks.title: Set up third-party Instagram scraper API
tasks.description: HUMAN ACTION REQUIRED: Sign up for a RapidAPI account at rapidapi.com. Subscribe to an Instagram scraper API (e.g. 'Instagram Scraper' or 'Instagram API' on RapidAPI). Obtain an API key. Store as EXPO_PUBLIC_RAPIDAPI_KEY in the .env file. Test the API manually to confirm it returns caption text, image URLs, and video URLs for public Instagram posts.
tasks.success_criteria: Human has a working RapidAPI key that returns structured post data (caption, image, video URL) for public Instagram posts.

tasks.title: Implement third-party scraper service and InstagramService facade
tasks.description: Create `src/services/instagram-scraper.ts` implementing `fetchViaScraper(url: string): Promise<RawInstagramPost | null>` using the RapidAPI key from env. Create `src/services/instagram.ts` as the facade: try scraper first (richer data), fall back to oEmbed, return null if both fail. Export a single `fetchInstagramPost(url: string): Promise<RawInstagramPost | null>` function. Add a `isVideoPost` flag to RawInstagramPost. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: All three service files exist, compile, and the facade correctly chains the two providers with fallback logic.

tasks.title: Implement regex recipe parser (no-API fallback)
tasks.description: Create `src/services/recipe-parser.ts`. Implement `parseRecipeFromCaption(caption: string): Partial<Recipe>`. Use regex/heuristics to detect common recipe patterns: lines starting with numbers/bullets as instructions, lines with measurements (cup, tsp, tbsp, g, oz, ml) as ingredients, first capitalised sentence as title. Also export `hasRecipeContent(caption: string): boolean` to check if a caption likely contains recipe data (presence of measurement words, 'ingredients', 'instructions', 'steps', 'method'). Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Parser file exists and compiles. hasRecipeContent returns true for a sample recipe caption and false for a non-recipe caption.

tasks.title: Set up Claude API key
tasks.description: HUMAN ACTION REQUIRED: Obtain a Claude API key from console.anthropic.com. Store it as EXPO_PUBLIC_CLAUDE_API_KEY in the .env file. Verify the key works by running a simple test call to the Claude API (claude-opus-4-6 model). Note: the API key will also be configurable at runtime via the app's Settings screen so end-users can provide their own key.
tasks.success_criteria: Human has a working Claude API key stored in .env. A test API call succeeds.

tasks.title: Implement Claude AI caption parser (Tier 1)
tasks.description: Create `src/services/recipe-parser-ai.ts`. Implement `parseRecipeWithAI(caption: string, apiKey: string): Promise<Partial<Recipe> | null>`. Use `@anthropic-ai/sdk` to call claude-opus-4-6. System prompt instructs Claude to extract recipe data from an Instagram caption and return ONLY a valid JSON object matching the Recipe type (title, description, ingredients array, instructions array, tags, prepTime, cookTime, servings). Parse the JSON response. Return null on API error. Also export `isExtractionSufficient(partial: Partial<Recipe>): boolean` â€” returns true if both ingredients.length > 0 and instructions.length > 0. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Service file compiles. Function signature is correct. Claude is called with the right model ID and a well-structured prompt.

tasks.title: Implement video frame extractor and Claude Vision parser (Tier 2)
tasks.description: Create `src/services/video-extractor.ts`. Implement `extractFrames(videoUri: string, frameCount?: number): Promise<string[]>` using `expo-video-thumbnails` â€” extract frameCount (default 6) frames evenly spaced across the video duration. Returns array of local file URIs. Implement `parseRecipeFromVideoFrames(frameUris: string[], apiKey: string): Promise<Partial<Recipe> | null>` â€” read each frame as base64 via expo-file-system, build a Claude API request with all frames as image content blocks plus a text prompt asking Claude to extract recipe data visible in the video frames. Return parsed JSON matching Recipe type. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Service file compiles. extractFrames and parseRecipeFromVideoFrames are exported. Claude Vision request is correctly structured with base64 image content blocks.

tasks.title: Build Add Recipe screen â€” URL input and fetch flow
tasks.description: Implement the first half of `app/add-recipe.tsx`: render URLInput component, handle the 'Fetch Recipe' action â€” show a loading spinner while calling fetchInstagramPost(), then calling parseRecipeWithAI() (Tier 1), then if insufficient, calling extractFrames() + parseRecipeFromVideoFrames() (Tier 2), then falling back to parseRecipeFromCaption(). Store the partial recipe in local component state. Show an error message if all methods fail. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Screen compiles. The fetch â†’ Tier 1 â†’ Tier 2 â†’ fallback chain is wired up. Loading and error states are handled.

tasks.title: Build Add Recipe screen â€” preview, edit, and save flow
tasks.description: Complete `app/add-recipe.tsx`: after fetch+parse, show a preview/edit form with editable fields for title, ingredients (add/remove/edit), instructions (add/remove/edit). Show the post image if available. Add a 'Save Recipe' button that calls recipeStore.addRecipe() with a generated UUID and timestamps. Navigate back to Home on success. Add a 'Skip â€” Enter Manually' option that bypasses fetch and shows a blank form. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Full add-recipe screen compiles. Save action adds recipe to Zustand store. Manual entry path works.

tasks.title: Build RecipeCard component
tasks.description: Create `src/components/RecipeCard.tsx`. Props: `recipe: Recipe`, `onPress: () => void`. Render a card with: hero image (expo-image with placeholder), recipe title (truncated to 2 lines), author, prep/cook time badges if present, first 2 ingredient names as a preview line. Style with NativeWind â€” rounded corners, shadow, consistent aspect ratio. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Component file exists, compiles, all props are typed, no inline styles (NativeWind className only).

tasks.title: Build Home screen with recipe grid
tasks.description: Implement `app/(tabs)/index.tsx`. Use `useRecipeStore()` to read recipes. Render a 2-column FlatList of RecipeCard components. Tapping a card navigates to `/recipe/[id]`. Add a FAB (floating action button) in the bottom-right corner that navigates to `/add-recipe`. Add a search bar at the top that filters recipes by title (client-side). Show an empty state (illustration + text) when no recipes are saved. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Screen renders recipe grid from store. FAB navigates to add-recipe. Search filters the list. Empty state shows when store is empty.

tasks.title: Build IngredientList and InstructionList components
tasks.description: Create `src/components/IngredientList.tsx`: renders a list of Ingredient items each with a checkbox. Checking an ingredient toggles its `checked` state and shows strikethrough text. Accepts `ingredients: Ingredient[]` and `onToggle: (index: number) => void` props. Create `src/components/InstructionList.tsx`: renders numbered steps. Each step has a circle number badge and text. Accepts `instructions: Instruction[]`. Style both with NativeWind. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Both component files exist and compile. IngredientList handles toggle state. InstructionList shows step numbers.

tasks.title: Build Recipe Detail screen
tasks.description: Implement `app/recipe/[id].tsx`. Read the recipe id from route params, look it up in recipeStore. Show: full-width hero image, title, author, extractionSource badge (caption/video/manual), prep/cook/servings row, IngredientList (with toggle persisted back to store), InstructionList. Add a header right button with options: 'Add to Board', 'Delete'. Delete shows an Alert confirmation then calls recipeStore.deleteRecipe() and navigates back. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Screen renders all recipe fields. Ingredient toggle updates store. Delete flow works with confirmation.

tasks.title: Build BoardCard component and Boards list screen
tasks.description: Create `src/components/BoardCard.tsx`. Props: `board: Board`, `recipeCount: number`, `onPress: () => void`. Show cover image (first recipe image or placeholder), board name, recipe count. Implement `app/(tabs)/boards.tsx`: list all boards in a 2-column grid using BoardCard. Add a 'New Board' button that shows an Alert prompt for the board name, then calls boardStore.addBoard(). Empty state for no boards. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: BoardCard component compiles. Boards screen shows boards from store. New board creation works via Alert prompt.

tasks.title: Build Board Detail screen and Add-to-Board flow
tasks.description: Implement `app/board/[id].tsx`: shows board name as title, grid of RecipeCard for recipes in the board (join by recipeIds). Long-press a recipe card to remove it from the board. Add an edit button to rename the board. Delete board option in header. In `app/recipe/[id].tsx`, wire up the 'Add to Board' option: show a Modal listing all boards with checkboxes for which boards this recipe belongs to. Tapping a board calls boardStore.addRecipeToBoard / removeRecipeFromBoard. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Board detail screen shows correct recipes. Remove from board works. Add-to-board modal shows and updates store.

tasks.title: Build Settings screen
tasks.description: Implement `app/(tabs)/settings.tsx`. Sections: (1) API Keys â€” a masked TextInput for Claude API key and an optional RapidAPI key. Values stored in MMKV directly (not Zustand, as they are sensitive). (2) App Info â€” version from app.json, link to GitHub repo. (3) Data â€” 'Export all recipes as JSON' button (uses expo-sharing), 'Clear all data' destructive button with confirmation. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: Settings screen compiles. API key inputs save to MMKV and are read back correctly. Clear data shows confirmation before deleting.

tasks.title: Add empty states across all screens
tasks.description: Create `src/components/EmptyState.tsx` with props: `icon: string` (emoji), `title: string`, `subtitle: string`, `actionLabel?: string`, `onAction?: () => void`. Use it in: Home screen (no recipes), Boards screen (no boards), Board Detail (board is empty), Recipe search (no results). Each instance should have contextually appropriate copy. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: EmptyState component exists and is used in all 4 locations. Each has unique copy appropriate to that screen.

tasks.title: Implement image caching with expo-file-system
tasks.description: Create `src/utils/image-cache.ts`. Implement `cacheImage(remoteUrl: string, recipeId: string): Promise<string>` â€” downloads the image to `FileSystem.documentDirectory + 'images/' + recipeId + '.jpg'` if not already cached. Returns the local URI. Implement `getCachedImageUri(recipeId: string): string | null` â€” returns the local URI if it exists, null otherwise. Update `recipeStore.addRecipe()` to call cacheImage() and store the localImageUri on the recipe. Update RecipeCard and Recipe Detail to prefer localImageUri over imageUrl. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: image-cache.ts exists. addRecipe downloads and caches the image. RecipeCard uses localImageUri when available.

tasks.title: Add error handling and retry logic
tasks.description: Create `src/utils/retry.ts` with a `withRetry<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>, maxAttempts?: number, delayMs?: number): Promise<T>` helper. Apply it to Instagram fetch calls in instagram.ts (max 3 attempts, 1s delay). Add a global error boundary component `src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx` wrapping the root layout. Add user-visible error toasts/alerts in the Add Recipe screen for each failure mode: network error, post not found, recipe not detected, API key missing. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: retry.ts and ErrorBoundary.tsx exist and compile. Fetch functions use withRetry. Error states in add-recipe screen have descriptive messages.

tasks.title: TypeScript audit and code cleanup
tasks.description: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and fix all TypeScript errors. Run `npx prettier --write .` and commit formatting changes. Check that all Zustand stores have correct generic types. Ensure no `any` types are used. Check that all expo-router Link/router.push calls use correct typed routes. Commit.
tasks.success_criteria: `npx tsc --noEmit` exits with 0 errors. No `any` types in src/ files.

tasks.title: Design and add app icon and splash screen
tasks.description: HUMAN ACTION REQUIRED: Design an app icon (1024x1024 PNG) and splash screen image. Place them in assets/. Update app.json to reference them under `expo.icon` and `expo.splash.image`. Run `npx expo-doctor` to verify asset configuration. Commit assets and updated app.json.
tasks.success_criteria: assets/icon.png (1024x1024) and assets/splash.png exist. app.json references them correctly.

tasks.title: End-to-end integration testing on real device
tasks.description: HUMAN ACTION REQUIRED: Run the app on a real iOS or Android device using `npx expo start`. Test the following flows: (1) paste a caption-based Instagram recipe link and verify Tier 1 extraction works, (2) paste a video-only recipe link and verify Tier 2 frame extraction works, (3) save a recipe, close and reopen app â€” verify persistence, (4) create a board, add recipes to it, (5) test Settings API key save/load. Log any bugs found and create GitHub issues.
tasks.success_criteria: All 5 test flows pass on a real device. No crash-level bugs. Recipe data persists across app restart.
